h1. Graph Projection and Neo4j Builder

h2. Purpose

Build a Neo4j graph projection from canonical Data Compass metadata for lineage, relationship traversal, impact analysis, and GraphRAG.

h2. Design Principle

{quote}
Neo4j is not the source of truth. It is a derived, rebuildable graph projection from MongoDB/canonical metadata.
{quote}

h2. Graph Nodes

* Node
* Dataset
* Distribution
* DataModelEntity
* DataModelAttribute
* DataService
* DataProduct
* DataFlow
* DataProcess
* Report

h2. Graph Relationships

|| Relationship || Meaning ||
| IMPLEMENTS | Distribution implements Dataset |
| CONTAINS | Entity contains Attribute |
| EXPOSES | DataService exposes Distribution |
| CONSUMES | Product/Process consumes Asset |
| PRODUCES | Product/Process produces Asset |
| DEPENDS_ON | Report depends on Asset |
| UPSTREAM_OF | Lineage upstream relationship |
| DOWNSTREAM_OF | Lineage downstream relationship |
| OWNS | Owner relationship |
| BELONGS_TO | Domain/product grouping |
| MAPS_TO | Attribute maps to DataElement |
| CLASSIFIED_AS | Asset has classification |

h2. Primary Requirements

|| ID || Requirement || Priority ||
| GRF-001 | Define Neo4j graph schema | P0 |
| GRF-002 | Create constraints and indexes | P0 |
| GRF-003 | Load Distribution/Dataset nodes | P0 |
| GRF-004 | Load Attribute nodes | P0 |
| GRF-005 | Load DataService relationships | P1 |
| GRF-006 | Load DataFlow lineage | P1 |
| GRF-007 | Load DataProcess lineage | P1 |
| GRF-008 | Build upstream/downstream traversal queries | P1 |

h2. Success Criteria

* Graph nodes reconcile with MongoDB source counts.
* Upstream traversal works by JRN.
* Downstream traversal works by JRN.
* Lineage paths can be returned with source references.
* Graph can be rebuilt from source metadata.

h2. Pitfalls / Risks

|| Risk || Impact || Mitigation ||
| Neo4j becomes competing source of truth | Conflicting answers | Treat graph as rebuildable projection |
| Missing JRN | Broken graph edges | Validate JRN before graph load |
| Duplicate nodes | Incorrect traversals | Enforce uniqueness constraints |
| Excessive traversal depth | Slow queries/noisy answers | Apply depth limits and filters |
